pageTitle.value: Orange River-Karoo Conservation Area - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: The Orange River-Karoo Conservation Area (ORKCA) is a project for creating a conservation corridor where landscape, wildlife and community can thrive.

metadescription.value: The Orange River-Karoo Conservation Area (ORKCA) is a project for creating a conservation corridor where landscape, wildlife and community can thrive. Learn more on rolex.org

text.value: A first-of-its-kind conservation project in Southern Namibia is on track to create one of the largest rewilding schemes on the African continent. Following two years of meticulous planning, the Orange River-Karoo Conservation Area (ORKCA) has translocated wildlife for the first time. With support from our Perpetual Planet Initiative, springbok, oryx and ostrich have been released onto the land – taking ORKCA one step closer to restoring this once-thriving ecosystem.

pageCaption.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

metadescription.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

pageCaption.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

metadescription.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - A vanished forest reborn

pageCaption.value: Working with dedicated farming families, Laury Cullen Jr. is restoring Brazil’s Atlantic Forest while contributing to the local economy and helping to fight climate change.

metadescription.value: Laury Cullen Jr.’s Dream Map initiative has overseen the planting of four million trees in Brazil’s Atlantic Forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rewilding Chile and Rewilding Argentina - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Thirty years ago, Kristine Tompkins and Douglas Tompkins left the corporate world to start a trailblazing conservation journey, rebuilding ecosystems in Argentina and Chile. Today, the work initiated by Tompkins Conservation is being continued by offspring organizations Rewilding Chile and Rewilding Argentina. Their mission is to restore and rebuild the natural world.

WaPageTitle.value: Tompkins Conservation: Rewilding Chile and Rewilding Argentina - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Rewilding Chile and Rewilding Argentina have enabled the restoration of millions of hectares of land in South America. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Cassai River Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative partner and National Geographic Explorer Steve Boyes has undertaken a journey like no other, as part of his Great Spine of Africa Expeditions.

WaPageTitle.value: Cassai River Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is revealing just how valuable the Angolan Highlands water tower is for the Congo River basin. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Cassai River – Episode 1

pageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Inza Koné - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: As a 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate, Inza Koné is protecting one of Côte d’Ivoire’s last remaining primary rainforests, Tanoé-Ehy. The forest teems with life, including species found nowhere else on Earth.

WaPageTitle.value: 2023 Rolex Awards Laureate - Inza Koné - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Primatologist Inza Koné has devoted his life to advocating for the endemic primates of Côte d'Ivoire's Tanoé-Ehy forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.
